prompt:
Operations brief, garagedeck program. We run a four-level parking structure and the booth terminal we rent is a black box we are cutting away from: it rounds with floats, it reads the wall clock mid-transaction, and its end-of-day report has never once reconciled with the cash drawer. You are building the replacement core, offline-testable and exactly reproducible. Plain JDK only, no external libraries, every class in the default package next to TestMain.java; graders run `java TestMain.java` (the multi-file source launcher compiles the rest on demand). TestMain.java is the signed-off acceptance suite and is read-only. The eight module files are already laid out as documented skeletons - implement them in place, one top-level class per file: VehicleClass, SpaceInventory, Ticket, TicketDesk, Pricing, Validations, Garage, DayReport.

HOUSE RULES.
- Logical clock: a tick is one minute on the deck clock, passed into every operation as a `long`. Never read wall time (System.currentTimeMillis, System.nanoTime, java.time clocks) - night audit replays a whole day of operations and the output must be byte-identical every run.
- Money is integer cents in `long`, end to end. No float or double anywhere near an amount.
- Anything locale-sensitive formats through java.util.Locale.ROOT.
- Do not use the `assert` keyword anywhere; the suite runs without -ea and checks with plain conditionals. Guard with explicit throws instead.
- Every failure below is an unchecked exception with the EXACT type and message shown; the booth UI matches on those strings. Placeholders like T-0009 / X-99 / CAFE / STANDARD stand for the offending id, code, or enum name.

1. VehicleClass.java - enum MOTORCYCLE, COMPACT, STANDARD, OVERSIZE, declared smallest to largest.
   - `String code()`: "M", "C", "S", "V" (V as in van - a printed "O" reads as zero on paper tickets).
   - `boolean fitsIn(VehicleClass spaceClass)`: a vehicle fits a space of its own class or any larger class. MOTORCYCLE fits all four; OVERSIZE fits only OVERSIZE.

2. SpaceInventory.java - the numbered spaces.
   - `SpaceInventory(int motorcycle, int compact, int standard, int oversize)` - space count per class. Space ids are `{code}-{number}`, number 1-based and zero-padded to two digits: M-01, M-02, ..., C-01, ...
   - `String assign(VehicleClass vehicle)` - walk the classes smallest to largest, skip classes the vehicle does not fit, and take the LOWEST-NUMBERED free space of the first fitting class that has one; mark it occupied and return its id. Return null when nothing fits (the caller decides whether that is an error). A COMPACT prefers C, then S, then V; it never takes M.
   - `void release(String spaceId)` - free the space. An id that does not exist in this inventory: IllegalArgumentException "unknown space X-99". A known id that is currently free: IllegalStateException "space M-01 is not occupied".
   - `boolean isOccupied(String spaceId)` - unknown ids throw the same IllegalArgumentException.
   - `int total(VehicleClass c)` / `int free(VehicleClass c)` - capacity and currently-free count for that class row.

3. Ticket.java - the record of one ticket, plus `enum Status { OPEN, PAID, VOID }` nested inside Ticket.
   - Accessors: `String id()`, `String plate()`, `VehicleClass vehicleClass()`, `String spaceId()`, `long entryTick()`, `Status status()`. A fresh ticket is OPEN. Status changes go through TicketDesk, not through a public setter.

4. TicketDesk.java - issue and track.
   - `Ticket issue(String plate, VehicleClass vehicleClass, String spaceId, long entryTick)` - ids run "T-0001", "T-0002", ... per desk (4-digit zero-padded serial).
   - `Ticket lookup(String id)` - IllegalArgumentException "unknown ticket T-0009" when absent.
   - `void markPaid(String id)` / `void voidTicket(String id)` - only an OPEN ticket may transition; on a PAID or VOID ticket: IllegalStateException "ticket T-0001 is not open". The unknown-ticket check comes first.
   - `java.util.List<Ticket> byStatus(Ticket.Status status)` - all tickets with that status, sorted by ticket id ascending.

5. Pricing.java - the posted tariff; static methods only.
   Rate card in cents, per class (first hour / each additional started hour / daily max):
     MOTORCYCLE 300 / 200 / 1500
     COMPACT 500 / 350 / 2500
     STANDARD 600 / 400 / 3000
     OVERSIZE 1000 / 700 / 5000
   - `static long charge(VehicleClass vehicleClass, long minutes)`:
     * minutes < 0: IllegalArgumentException "negative duration".
     * Split into full days d = minutes / 1440 and remainder r = minutes % 1440.
     * Every full day bills the daily max.
     * The remainder bills started hours h = ceil(r / 60) on the tier firstHour + (h - 1) * additionalHour, CAPPED at the daily max. When d == 0 the first hour is due even at r == 0 (the hour starts when the ticket prints); when d > 0 and r == 0 the remainder adds nothing.
     * Worked, all pinned by the suite: STANDARD 0 min -> 600. STANDARD 61 min -> 2 started hours -> 600 + 400 = 1000. STANDARD 480 min (8h) -> 600 + 7*400 = 3400, capped -> 3000. COMPACT 480 min -> 500 + 7*350 = 2950, capped -> 2500. STANDARD 1440 min -> exactly one day -> 3000. STANDARD 1441 min -> 3000 + first hour = 3600. STANDARD 1800 min -> 3000 + 6h (600 + 5*400 = 2600) = 5600. MOTORCYCLE 2280 min -> one day 1500 + 840 min = 14h -> 300 + 13*200 = 2900, capped at 1500 -> 3000 total.
   - `static String money(long cents)` - "$" + dollars + "." + two-digit cents via Locale.ROOT, no thousands separator: 0 -> "$0.00", 5 -> "$0.05", 1750 -> "$17.50", 100013 -> "$1000.13". Amounts in this system are never negative.

6. Validations.java - the merchant validation book.
   - `void defineFlat(String code, long cents)` - cents < 0: IllegalArgumentException "negative amount".
   - `void definePercent(String code, int percent)` - outside 0..100: IllegalArgumentException "percent must be 0..100".
   - Registering a code that already exists (either kind): IllegalArgumentException "validation CAFE already defined".
   - `boolean defined(String code)`.
   - `long apply(String code, long amountCents)` - the amount AFTER this one validation. Unknown code: IllegalArgumentException "unknown validation NOPE". A flat validation subtracts its cents; a percent validation computes its discount on the CURRENT amount with half-up integer rounding: discount = (amountCents * percent + 50) / 100. The result never drops below zero. Worked: 15% on 1750 -> discount (1750*15 + 50) / 100 = 263 (half-up from 262.5) -> 1487 remains; flat 5000 on 3000 -> 0.

7. Garage.java - the facade the booth drives; wires one SpaceInventory, one TicketDesk, one Validations book.
   - `Garage(int motorcycle, int compact, int standard, int oversize)`.
   - `Validations validations()` - the garage's book; merchant codes are defined through it.
   - `Ticket enter(String plate, VehicleClass vehicleClass, long tick)` - assign a space under the policy above; when assign returns null: IllegalStateException "no space available for STANDARD" (the enum name). Otherwise issue and return the ticket.
   - `void stamp(String ticketId, String code)` - record a validation on an OPEN ticket. Checks in order: unknown ticket (IllegalArgumentException, as lookup); not open (IllegalStateException "ticket T-0001 is not open"); unknown validation code (IllegalArgumentException "unknown validation NOPE"); same code already on the ticket (IllegalStateException "CAFE already stamped on T-0001"). Stamps keep their order. Stamping takes no tick - it never touches the clock.
   - `Garage.Receipt exit(String ticketId, long tick)` - settle an OPEN ticket. Unknown/not-open checks as above; then tick < entryTick: IllegalArgumentException "exit before entry". minutes = tick - entryTick; base = Pricing.charge(class, minutes); then apply the ticket's stamps IN STAMP ORDER, each to the running amount - order changes the answer: base 2500 with CINEMA 25% then CAFE flat 500 gives 2500 -> 1875 -> 1375, the same stamps reversed give 2500 -> 2000 -> 1500. Mark the ticket PAID, release its space, file the receipt and return it.
   - `void voidTicket(String ticketId)` - void an OPEN ticket (desk rules apply) and release its space; no charge, no receipt.
   - `Receipt` is a nested class `Garage.Receipt`: `String ticketId()`, `long minutes()`, `long baseCents()`, `long dueCents()`, `long discountCents()` (= base - due), `java.util.List<String> appliedValidations()` (stamp order).
   - Accessors for the report and the booth: `Ticket lookup(String ticketId)`; `Receipt receiptFor(String ticketId)` - nothing filed (the ticket never exited): IllegalArgumentException "no receipt for T-0001"; `java.util.List<Ticket> ticketsByStatus(Ticket.Status status)`; `int freeSpaces(VehicleClass c)`; `int occupied(VehicleClass c)`; `int capacity(VehicleClass c)`.

8. DayReport.java - `static String render(Garage garage, long tick)`, the end-of-day reconciliation export, character-exact. Every line ends with '\n' (the last one too); detail lines are indented two spaces; every ticket group is sorted by ticket id ascending; the vehicle class prints as the enum name; every amount goes through Pricing.money. Template:
   == reconciliation @ tick {tick} ==
   open tickets: {count}
     {id} {plate} {CLASS} {spaceId} in={entryTick}
   paid tickets: {count}
     {id} {plate} {CLASS} due {due} (base {base}, validated {discount})
   voided tickets: {count}
     {id} {plate} {CLASS}
   occupancy: M {occupied}/{capacity} C {occupied}/{capacity} S {occupied}/{capacity} V {occupied}/{capacity}
   revenue: base {baseTotal}, validated {discountTotal}, due {dueTotal}
   The revenue line totals PAID tickets only - voids contribute nothing. A group with zero tickets still prints its header line with count 0 and no detail lines. An empty day at a 1/1/1/1 garage rendered at tick 0 is exactly:
   == reconciliation @ tick 0 ==
   open tickets: 0
   paid tickets: 0
   voided tickets: 0
   occupancy: M 0/1 C 0/1 S 0/1 V 0/1
   revenue: base $0.00, validated $0.00, due $0.00
   And one populated paid line, for a compact that parked 8 hours (base capped at $25.00) with a 25% cinema stamp then a $5.00 cafe stamp (2500 -> 1875 -> 1375):
     T-0001 AAA-111 COMPACT due $13.75 (base $25.00, validated $11.25)

Acceptance is `java TestMain.java` printing "0 failed" and exiting 0. The suite pins every string above character for character. The drawer-count meeting is Monday morning, and this time the report has to add up.
